title: HTTP 200 is not 'found' — the verdict is the worker's data.success / data.data.valid
summary: The worker posts every outcome (incl. not-found and unsupported-country) to /jobs/{id}/complete, never /fail, so a missed lookup is still status:completed / HTTP 200. There are two success flags: envelope.success/status = the JOB ran; data.success = the LOOKUP landed. search is always data.success:true (check total_results/results.length); lookup success:false carries data.error ('Company not found' / 'Unsupported country: XX') and no data.data; vat is always success:true with the verdict at data.data.valid. CRITICAL ambiguity: search swallows upstream UK/US API errors and returns success:true, results:[], total_results:0 — identical to a genuine no-match — so an empty search is worth ONE retry, then treat as no-match. Always read data.success/data.error before concluding; a confident wrong 'found it' off a success:false is the costliest agent error here.
references: app/api/v1/jobs/status.py:393-410 — /jobs/{id}/results returns raw worker response under data (no transform) for spiderCompanyData
windmill-scripts/spidercompanydata_lookup.py:403-433 — missing identifier / unsupported country / not-found all -> success:false posted to /complete
windmill-scripts/spidercompanydata_search.py:56-58 — UK API non-200 swallowed -> returns [] (success:true, total_results:0)
windmill-scripts/spidercompanydata_search.py:284-291 — search envelope success:true regardless of result count
